name: Alto de Valmala - Sierra de la Demanda Loop
name_original: Alto de Valmala por los pies de la Sierra de la Demanda — circular desde el Embalse de Úzquiza
description: This moderate 41.0-mile road cycling route from Embalse de Úzquiza climbs Alto de Valmala, offering scenic views of the Sierra de la Demanda
region_intro: Road cycling around Embalse de Úzquiza offers routes through a landscape characterized by the reservoir itself and the surrounding Sierra de la Demanda mountain range. The terrain includes ascents and descents typical of mountainous regions, providing varied challenges for road cyclists. Routes often feature views of the reservoir and pass through areas with natural scenery. Best road cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza The most popular road cycling route is Alto de Valmala - Sierra de la Demanda Loop, a 41.1 miles (66.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete, offering a moderate challenge through the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Viewpoint - Úzquiza Reservoir – Alto de Pradilla loop from Villasur de Herreros, a difficult 41.2 miles (66.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and scenic views around the reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Alarcia – Alto de Pradilla loop from Rábanos, a 45.1 miles (72.6 km) trail leading through varied mountain terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Embalse de Úzquiza is defined by reservoir views, mountain roads, and varied terrain. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Embalse de Úzquiza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Úzquiza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: The region around Embalse de Úzquiza, nestled within the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, features a varied terrain. Cyclists can expect a mix of ascents and descents, typical of mountainous areas, with routes often offering views of the reservoir and passing through natural scenery.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: While the area is known for its moderate to difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056673821/alto-de-valmala-sierra-de-la-demanda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Valmala - Sierra de la Demanda Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated moderate, there are no routes explicitly categorized as 'easy' in the provided data. Cyclists should be prepared for some elevation gain and challenging sections.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Embalse de Úzquiza area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint - Úzquiza Reservoir – Alto de Pradilla loop from Villasur de Herreros&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcia – Alto de Pradilla loop from Rábanos&lt;/a&gt; are rated as difficult. These routes feature significant elevation gains and cover distances of over 40 miles.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056673821/alto-de-valmala-sierra-de-la-demanda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Valmala - Sierra de la Demanda Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pineda de la Sierra – Puerto del Manquillo loop from Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for road cycling routes here?
A: The road cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza typically range from approximately 27 miles (44 km) to 45 miles (72 km) in length. Completion times vary depending on the route's difficulty and your pace, generally taking between 2.5 to 4 hours.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: Given its location in a mountainous region, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling around Embalse de Úzquiza, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations, especially in the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views, particularly of the Embalse de Úzquiza reservoir itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint - Úzquiza Reservoir – Alto de Pradilla loop from Villasur de Herreros&lt;/a&gt; explicitly highlights a viewpoint, suggesting excellent panoramic opportunities along its course.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: The road cycling routes in Embalse de Úzquiza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the beautiful reservoir and mountain views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, one route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site – Arlanzón loop from El Peñedo&lt;/a&gt;, passes by the renowned Atapuerca Archaeological Site. This offers a unique opportunity to combine road cycling with a visit to a significant historical landmark.

Q: What should I consider regarding parking when planning a road cycling trip here?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for each route, many routes start from villages or towns such as Villasur de Herreros, Rábanos, or Pineda de la Sierra. It is advisable to check local parking availability in these starting points before your ride.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Embalse de Úzquiza area?
A: There are 5 distinct road cycling routes detailed for the Embalse de Úzquiza area, offering a range of moderate to difficult challenges for cyclists.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on these routes?
A: The routes around Embalse de Úzquiza feature significant elevation gains, reflecting the mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcia – Alto de Pradilla loop from Rábanos&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1300 meters (4265 feet) of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint - Úzquiza Reservoir – Alto de Pradilla loop from Villasur de Herreros&lt;/a&gt; has over 1000 meters (3280 feet) of climbing.
tips: Alarcia is a town located in the province of Burgos. It forms part of the Montes de Oca region and is located between the Úzquiza reservoir, to the west, and the San Millán peak of the Sierra de la Demanda, to the southeast. A pretty town worth a visit.

It is a 4km climb on the BU-813 with an average gradient of 5.1%. As can be seen, the slope ranges between 4 and 6% without hard ramps (only one at 9%) and relatively constant with several horseshoe curves at the exit of the town but without excessive complication.

Before reaching the town of Tosantos we can find the valley that bears the name of the same river, Río Retorto. From this road you can see the formations created by the passage of water through the alluvial lands of this area.

On the N-120 between Villafranca Montes de Oca and Burgos there is a fork on the right that leads (heading north-west on the BU-V-7012 road) to Atapuerca. In this municipality there is an archaeological site of primary importance, containing a large quantity of hominid bones dating back to over 400,000 years ago. For this reason the site has been included among the World Heritage sites. 
The archaeological park can be visited from Monday to Friday (from 9 to 17), Saturdays and public holidays (from 9 to 14)

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 66.1 kilometers long with an elevation gain of about 800 meters. It typically takes around 3 hours and 2 minutes to complete, assuming a moderate pace.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate for road cycling, meaning it requires good fitness. While the climb up Alto de Valmala is manageable, it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Riders should have some experience with road cycling and climbing.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from the Embalse de Úzquiza. You can find parking facilities near the reservoir to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. The main climb, Alto de Valmala, is a 4-kilometer ascent on the BU-813 road with an average gradient of 5.1% and only one short ramp reaching 9%.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll experience beautiful Castilian landscape, traversing the foothills of the Sierra de la Demanda. The route offers scenic views, charming villages like Alarcia, and passes through the Retorto River Valley. While specific wildlife sightings aren't guaranteed, the rural setting of Castilla y León often provides opportunities to observe local fauna.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but be mindful of potential heat, especially during midday. Winter might bring colder temperatures and possible snow in higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, key highlights include the village of Alarcia, the challenging yet rewarding climb up Alto de Valmala, and the picturesque Retorto River Valley. You'll also enjoy the expansive Castilian landscape throughout the ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Camino Francés - 03 Logroño a Burgos, Alto Oca, Etapa 4 Villafranca Montes de Oca - Ibeas de Juarros, and Etapa 2 Alarcia - Pradoluengo.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on paved roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a road bike ride of this distance and difficulty is typically not recommended for their safety or comfort. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer designed for pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the Sierra de la Demanda area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general area of the Sierra de la Demanda where it is located. It's a public road cycling route.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near the starting point?
faqs.answer: While the route starts from the Embalse de Úzquiza, you'll find more amenities in nearby villages. It's advisable to check for specific establishments in towns like Alarcia or other larger settlements in the vicinity of the Sierra de la Demanda for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation before or after your ride.
